nameMeanings.name: Flambard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 Norman, English: nickname from Old French flambard ‘flaming torch’. There is no evidence to support an origin in a Continental Germanic personal name.
2  French: nickname from the same origin as (1).

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Eleanor Flambard was born in Cambridgeshire, England as the daughter of John Edmund Flambard and Lady Elizabeth Fitz Ralph. She married Thomas Tyrrell in 1356, in England. She died on 29 March 1422, in Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Eleanor Flambard est née à Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, fille de John Edmund Flambard et Lady Elizabeth Fitz Ralph. Elle a épousé Thomas Tyrrell en 1356, à Angleterre. Elle est décédée le 29 mars 1422, à Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Eleanor Flambard wurde in Cambridgeshire, England, als Tochter von John Edmund Flambard und Lady Elizabeth Fitz Ralph geboren. Sie hat 1356 in England Thomas Tyrrell geheiratet. Sie starb am 29. März 1422 in Essex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Eleanor Flambard è nata il nel Cambridgeshire, England come figlia di John Edmund Flambard e Lady Elizabeth Fitz Ralph. Lei sposato Thomas Tyrrell nel 1356, nel England. Morì il 29 marzo 1422, nel Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor FlambardはイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアでJohn Edmund FlambardとLady Elizabeth Fitz Ralphの娘として生まれた。1356年にイギリスでThomas Tyrrellと結婚した。彼女は1422年3月29日にイギリスエセックスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor Flambard(은)는 영국 캄브리지실르에서 John Edmund Flambard(와)과 Lady Elizabeth Fitz Ralph의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1356년 영국에서 Thomas Tyrrell(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1422년 3월 29일 영국 에세스에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Eleanor Flambard nasceu em Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra filha de John Edmund Flambard e Lady Elizabeth Fitz Ralph. Ela casou-se com Thomas Tyrrell em 1356, em Inglaterra. Ela faleceu em 29 de março de 1422, em Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Eleanor Flambard родилась в местности камбриджешир, Англия. Её родителей звали John Edmund Flambard и Lady Elizabeth Fitz Ralph. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Tyrrell в 1356 г. в местности Англия. Она умерла 29 марта 1422 г. в местности эссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Eleanor Flambard nació en Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra como hija de John Edmund Flambard y Lady Elizabeth Fitz Ralph. Se casó con Thomas Tyrrell en 1356, en Inglaterra. Murió el 29 de marzo de 1422, en Essex, Inglaterra.
